title: What Are Data Libraries?
chunk_text:
Key Concepts

- Agentforce Data Libraries
- Trusted data sources
- Agent grounding
- AI response accuracy
- Data 360
- Search indexes
- Retrievers

title: What Are Data Libraries?
chunk_text:
Source Content

Agentforce Data Libraries enhance the accuracy of AI features like Agentforce Agents by connecting them to trusted data sources.

Data libraries help agents use approved business information instead of relying only on the model's general knowledge.

A data library can be used to ground an agent's responses in selected source content.

title: What Are Data Libraries?
chunk_text:
Exam Relevance

This source supports the Data 360 Fundamentals domain of the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist exam.

It is relevant because the exam guide includes Agentforce Data Library concepts, chunking, indexing, and retrievers as Data 360 Fundamentals topics.

title: What Are Data Libraries?
chunk_text:
Notes

This source should be paired with additional Salesforce Help sources about choosing a data source, setting up data libraries, search indexes, and retrievers.

title: Setting Up Data Libraries
chunk_text:
Summary

Agentforce Data Libraries require Data 360 setup. When a data library is configured, the selected source content is indexed in Data Cloud, where a search index and retriever are automatically created.

title: Setting Up Data Libraries
chunk_text:
Key Concepts

- Agentforce Data Libraries
- Data 360 setup
- Knowledge articles
- File uploads
- Web search
- Custom retrievers
- Data Cloud indexing
- Search indexes
- Retrievers
- Answer Questions with Knowledge action

title: Setting Up Data Libraries
chunk_text:
Source Content

To use Agentforce Data Libraries, Data 360 must be set up.

When configuring a data library, supported data sources include knowledge articles, file uploads, web search, and custom retrievers.

The selected data source is indexed in Data Cloud.

A search index and retriever are automatically created for the data library.

When integrated with AI agents, the data library's search index and retriever are used by the Answer Questions with Knowledge action.

Data libraries can be created and assigned to features from Agentforce Data Library setup or when configuring an agent in Agentforce Builder.

title: Setting Up Data Libraries
chunk_text:
Exam Relevance

This source supports the Data 360 Fundamentals domain of the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist exam.

It is relevant because the exam guide includes Data 360 concepts such as chunking, indexing, and retrievers. It also explains how data libraries support grounded agent responses through indexed source content and retriever-backed knowledge actions.

title: Setting Up Data Libraries
chunk_text:
Notes

Use this source for questions about data library setup, supported data sources, automatic search index creation, automatic retriever creation, and how agents use data libraries through the Answer Questions with Knowledge action.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_heading: About the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam
chunk_text:
About the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam

The Salesforce Agentforce Specialist Certification is for individuals who design and implement
prompt templates and agents. The exam validates a candidate's ability to build agents that reason
and act across Salesforce and external channels. It targets Administrators, Developers, and
Architects who configure Prompt Builder for grounding, leverage Data 360 for retrieval, and manage
the Development Lifecycle of agents from testing to production.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_heading: Audience Description: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist
chunk_text:
Audience Description: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist

The Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist candidate is proficient in configuring and managing
AI agents and prompt templates on the Salesforce Platform. The candidate has a comprehensive
understanding of Salesforce Platform configuration and the ability to align AI agents to complex
business use cases. Successful candidates demonstrate the skills to deliver personalized,
context-aware interactions using Agentforce tools and capabilities.

A successful candidate typically has 1 year of experience with Salesforce platform configuration and
standard objects, including Data 360. This includes hands-on experience building agents in
Agentforce Agent Builder and creating prompt templates in Prompt Builder. Furthermore, the candidate
is familiar with testing tools such as Agentforce Testing Center and established deployment patterns
from Sandbox to Production.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_heading: A candidate for this exam has the knowledge and skills to:
chunk_text:
A candidate for this exam has the knowledge and skills to:

- Engineer Agentforce agents using next-generation authoring (NGA).
- Manage the Agent and Prompt Template Lifecycle, including ideation, building, testing, deployment,
  and observation.
- Implement sophisticated grounding for agents and prompt templates using Data 360 concepts such as
  chunking, indexing, and retrievers.
- Maintain governance by applying the Trust Layer and managing specific model access.
- Orchestrate systems using existing open standard multi-agent protocols, including Model Context
  Protocol (MCP) and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication.
- Determine appropriate architectures, such as Single-Agent (SOMA), to ensure scalability and
  control.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_heading: A candidate for this certification is not expected to:
chunk_text:
A candidate for this certification is not expected to:

- Have extensive knowledge of configuring and fine-tuning large language models (LLMs).
- Understand the basics of coding languages such as Apex or Python.
- Possess expertise in external AI tools.
- Understand transformer architecture.
- Perform return on investment (ROI) calculations or white space analysis.
- Have experience with Marketing Cloud, Heroku, Commerce Cloud, MuleSoft, Tableau, Slack, Quip, or
  Industry Clouds.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
Purpose of This Exam Guide

This exam guide is designed to help you prepare for the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist
Exam. This guide provides information about the target audience, the recommended training and
documentation, and a complete list of exam objectives. Salesforce highly recommends a combination of
on-the-job experience and self-study to maximize your chances of passing the exam.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
About the Exam

Here are details about the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam.

- Content: 60 multiple-choice questions and up to five unscored questions
- Time allotted to complete the exam: 105 minutes
- Passing score: 72%
- Version: Exam questions align to the Spring ’26 release.
- Prerequisite: None

This exam may contain up to five additional unscored questions to gather performance data. Unscored
questions are randomly integrated and have no impact on your final exam result.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
Exam Domain Weight Summary

The highest-weighted domain on the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Spring 2026 exam is
**AI Agents**, which accounts for **35%** of the exam.

The exam domain weights are:

1. **AI Agents: 35%**
2. **Prompt Engineering: 20%**
3. **Data 360 Fundamentals: 20%**
4. **Testing, Deployment, & Maintenance: 10%**
5. **Governance & Observability: 10%**
6. **Multi-Agent Orchestration: 5%**

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
**Prompt Engineering: 20%**

- Given business requirements, identify when it's appropriate to use Prompt Builder.
- Identify access controls governing prompt templates.
- Identify the considerations for using a prompt template type such as field generation and flex
  types.
- Given a scenario, identify the appropriate grounding technique.
- Explain the process for creating, activating, and executing prompt templates.
- Explain how to implement best practices for writing effective prompts.
- Identify the security and privacy features of the Trust Layer.
- Explain how to manage and prevent specific models from being accessed.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
**Data 360 Fundamentals: 20%**

- Explain the considerations of Agentforce Data Library and its concepts.
- Explain foundational concepts of Data 360 such as chunking, indexing, and retrievers.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
**AI Agents: 35%**

- Explain how an agent works and its basic building blocks of agent script.
- Explain the components and benefits of hybrid reasoning, including how Agent Script functions in
  Canvas and Script View.
- Given a use case, manage deterministic behavior for the agent using mechanisms like filters,
  variables, and template expressions.
- Given a scenario, select and configure standard topics, custom topics, standard Agent actions, and
  custom Agent actions.
- Explain the process for connecting agents to various channels such as digital experience, email,
  voice, and Slack.
- Explain the security context in which the agent is actually running, and how it impacts agent
  action execution.
- Given a scenario, identify when to use an Employee or Service agent.
- Given a scenario, identify when it's appropriate to use Agent API.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
**Testing, Deployment, & Maintenance: 10%**

- Given a scenario, test an agent using Testing Center.
- Explain how Testing Center evaluations work.
- Identify the considerations for deploying an agent from sandbox to production.
- Identify the considerations for deploying a template from sandbox to production.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
**Multi-Agent Orchestration: 5%**

- Given a scenario, determine whether a Single-Agent (SOMA) architecture is appropriate for
  scalability and control.
- Explain the purpose of existing open standard multi-agent protocols such as MCP and A2A.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_text:
Recommended Training and Resources

As preparation for this exam, we recommend a combination of hands-on experience, training course
completion, Trailhead Trails, and self-study in the areas listed in the Exam Outline section of this
exam guide.

title: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Exam Guide Spring 2026
chunk_heading: The self-study materials recommended for this exam include:
chunk_text:
The self-study materials recommended for this exam include:

- [Agentblazer Status](https://trailhead.salesforce.com/agentblazer)
- [Salesforce Certified Platform Administrator](https://trailhead.salesforce.com/en/credentials/administrator)
  - The Salesforce Certified Platform Administrator certification is designed for those who have
    experience with Salesforce and continuously look for ways to assist their companies in getting
    even more from additional features and capabilities.
- [Salesforce Certified Platform App Builder](https://trailhead.salesforce.com/en/credentials/platformappbuilder)
  - The Salesforce Certified Platform App Builder certification is designed for those who can
    demonstrate skills and knowledge in designing, building, and implementing custom applications
    using the declarative customization capabilities of the Salesforce Platform.

To review online documentation, tip sheets, and user guides, search for the topics listed in the
Exam Outline section of this guide on
[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com/s/?language=en_US) and study the information related
to those topics.

title: Retrieve Data
chunk_text:
Source Content

A retriever searches and returns relevant data from a search index.

Retrievers help make source-backed information available to prompts and agents.

A retriever is connected to a search index and controls what information is returned for a particular use case.

Retrievers can be created and customized in AI Models.

title: Retrieve Data
chunk_text:
Exam Relevance

This source supports the Data 360 Fundamentals domain of the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist exam.

It is relevant because the exam guide explicitly includes retrievers as part of Data 360 Fundamentals. It also supports questions about how retrieved source content is made available for grounded AI responses.

title: Retrieve Data
chunk_text:
Notes

Use this source for basic questions about what a retriever does, how it relates to a search index, and why retrievers matter for source-backed Agentforce responses.

title: Salesforce Spring '26 Release Notes Index
chunk_text:
Summary

The Salesforce Spring '26 release notes index is used as a release-currency control source.

This source helps identify when a topic may be release-sensitive and should be checked against Spring '26 documentation before relying on older Winter '25 or earlier guidance.

title: Salesforce Spring '26 Release Notes Index
chunk_text:
Key Concepts

- Spring '26 release notes
- Release readiness
- Release note changes
- Monthly feature releases
- Data 360 release notes
- Agentforce Platform release notes
- Feature availability
- Release updates
- Known issues

title: Salesforce Spring '26 Release Notes Index
chunk_text:
Source Content

The Spring '26 release notes provide links to Salesforce release readiness resources, release changes, release updates, and product-specific release note areas.

The release notes include a Release Note Changes page for updates made after the release notes were published.

The release notes include guidance on how and when features become available.

The release notes include a Data 360 section. Data 360 features and changes can be released as often as monthly, so Data 360 topics should be treated as release-sensitive.

The release notes include an Agentforce Platform section for predictive and generative AI updates.

The release notes identify Salesforce Known Issues as a separate source.

title: Salesforce Spring '26 Release Notes Index
chunk_text:
Exam Relevance

This source supports release-currency checks for the Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist Spring 2026 exam.

It should not be treated as the primary authority for exam scope. The exam guide remains the scope authority.

Use this source to flag topics that may have changed since Winter '25 or earlier, especially Data 360, Agentforce Platform, release updates, feature availability, and monthly feature changes.

title: Salesforce Spring '26 Release Notes Index
chunk_text:
Notes

Use this source as a freshness and release-control layer.

Do not use this source alone to answer detailed concept questions unless the question is specifically about Spring '26 release readiness, release-note changes, feature availability, monthly releases, or where to check for release-sensitive updates.
